Design Process Authenticity Evaluation

Evaluate the authenticity of design thinking processes

Welcome to the Design Process Evaluation Study

Thank you for participating in this research study. Your expertise as a designer will help us understand how authentic design processes are perceived and evaluated.

What You'll Do:

  • Evaluate 10 design process phases from real projects
  • Rate the authenticity of each designer's thinking process
  • Identify cognitive states present in their work
  • Estimated time: 20-30 minutes

Understanding Cognitive States in Design

During the design process, designers experience different cognitive states that influence their thinking and decision-making. You'll be asked to identify which of these states are present in each design phase:

Ambiguity

Multiple Interpretations

When information can be understood in several different ways, leading to multiple valid interpretations or solutions. The designer acknowledges that there isn't one "right" answer.

Vagueness

Unclear Boundaries

When concepts or requirements lack clear definition or precise boundaries. The designer works with fuzzy concepts that don't have exact specifications.

Uncertainty

Insufficient Information

When there's a lack of complete information or knowledge about the situation. The designer must make decisions despite not having all the facts.

Tacit Knowledge

Intuitive Understanding

When the designer relies on personal experience, intuition, or "gut feeling" that's difficult to explicitly articulate. This includes skills learned through practice.

Note: Multiple cognitive states can be present in a single design phase. Select all that apply based on what you observe.
